entries.title: Governance single-point-of-failure: no branch protection, no rulesets, no CODEOWNERS on a 32k-star repo that ships hooks into every Claude Code session (C20)
entries.action_hint: If you install caveman: pin to a specific release tag, not main. After install, check ~/.claude/hooks/ against the tagged release. If you maintain this repo: enable branch protection on main and add CODEOWNERS covering hooks/, rules/, .github/workflows/, install scripts.
entries.how_to_fix: GitHub *Settings → Branches → Add branch protection rule* for `main`: require pull request reviews (1 approver minimum), require status checks, require branches to be up to date before merging, do not allow bypassing. Also add a `.github/CODEOWNERS` file covering at minimum `hooks/`, `rules/`, `.github/workflows/`, and install scripts.

meta_table.label: Active executable surface
meta_table.value: Hooks in every Claude Code session

entries.threat_model_context: C20 escalates to Critical when the repo ships executable code to user machines AND has active code flow (≥ 1 release in the last 30 days). Caveman meets both: hook files run on every Claude Code session start and every user prompt; 10 releases (v1.0.0 → v1.6.0) shipped in the last 30 days.

entries.title: Unadvertised symlink vulnerability — named in release notes, not in advisory channel
entries.action_hint: If you installed before 2026-04-15, upgrade now. New installers on v1.6.0 are fine.
entries.how_to_fix: Upgrade to v1.6.0 via `claude plugin disable caveman && claude plugin install caveman`. Verify with `grep -c 'safeWriteFlag' ~/.claude/hooks/caveman-config.js` — should return >= 1.

meta_table.label: The fix
meta_table.value: O_NOFOLLOW + atomic temp+rename + symlink refusal at parent and target

entries.threat_model_context: Local privilege-escalation: attacker (or malicious process running as the user) replaces the flag file path with a symlink, and the hook overwrites the symlink target on the next Claude Code session.

entries.title: v1.6.0 was a 10+ PR batch merge — security fix hidden among unrelated changes
entries.action_hint: Ask the maintainer to stop batching security fixes with unrelated PRs.

entries.title: Review rate: 15% formal, 36% any (F11 dual metric)
entries.action_hint: Only relevant if you rely on caveman for sensitive workflows. Ask maintainer to require one other approver on hook/workflow PRs.

entries.title: No GitHub Security Advisory workflow — no way to learn about future vulns
entries.action_hint: Subscribe to the repo's releases — release notes are currently the only disclosure channel. Ask maintainer to enable advisories.

entries.title: No branch protection rule on main
entries.action_hint: No action required of you — this is a maintainer-side issue. Compounds every other finding though.

entries.title: v1.6.0 security code is thorough and well-reasoned
entries.action_hint: No action needed. Positive signal: maintainer's v1.6.0 fix shows real security engineering care.

entries.title: CI sync amplifies one rule file into 4+ always-on agent configs
entries.action_hint: No action needed today. Watch for future changes to rules/caveman-activate.md — leverage ratio is high.

meta_table.label: Leverage
meta_table.value: ⚠ High — 1 file → every user's always-on agent config

entries.action: Most urgent: if you installed caveman before 2026-04-15, upgrade now. New installers get v1.6.0 (fine). Ask maintainer to publish a Security Advisory and add SECURITY.md.

split_axis_decision.reasoning: The verdict splits on version: v1.6.0 code is clean, but v1.0.0–v1.5.1 had a live exploitable symlink vulnerability. Historical installers need to upgrade; new installers get the hardened version.

entries.audience: Version · Current release · v1.6.0 · installing today
entries.headline: Clean code, but no upstream gate — every install trusts the maintainer's credentials
entries.summary: Code in v1.6.0 is sound. But every install path fetches live main, and there is no branch protection on main, no rulesets, no CODEOWNERS (F0 Critical). A single compromised maintainer credential ships code to every Claude Code session on the next install.

entries.headline: Upgrade required
entries.summary: A local-privilege-escalation vulnerability shipped for 11 days. No advisory was issued. If you installed before 2026-04-15, upgrade now — do not skip. The F0 governance gap applies to historical installs too: even now, there is no upstream gate preventing a repeat.

selections.reason: Falsification: if the symlink attack vector didn't exist in the reporter's commit message or the fix diff didn't show the vulnerable write, F5 would not fire.
Falsification: if security-advisories API returned a non-empty array, the disclosure gap finding would close.
Falsification: if branch-protection endpoint returned 200 or rulesets were non-empty, the F0 Critical verdict would move to Warning or Clean.

paths: credential phishing against a 32k-star-repo maintainer (findable target)
malicious IDE extension running as the maintainer
sloppy review of an attacker PR — base review rate is 15% formal / 36% any

entries.headline: If you installed v1.5.1 or earlier, upgrade or remove now
entries.non_technical: Open Claude Code, run /plugin disable caveman, then /plugin install caveman. This gets you the hardened v1.6.0.
entries.command:
cat ~/.claude/hooks/caveman-config.js 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'safeWriteFlag'
claude plugin disable caveman
claude plugin install caveman

entries.headline: If installing for the first time, prefer plugin manager over curl-pipe and verify after
entries.non_technical: Installing now is fine — the hardened version is what you get. Be aware this plugin runs code every time Claude Code starts and every time you submit a prompt.

entries.headline: Ask the maintainer for standard hygiene
entries.non_technical: Open an issue asking for: a GitHub Security Advisory, SECURITY.md, Dependabot re-enabled, and branch protection on main.

entries.label: REPO CREATED
entries.note: Repo created by JuliusBrussee. v1.0.0 released same day — hooks ship with the symlink-unsafe flag write (unknown at the time).

entries.label: RAPID RELEASES
entries.note: v1.1.0 through v1.3.5 released over 5 days. All still contain the vulnerable hook write; nobody had reported it yet.

entries.label: VULN REPORTED
entries.note: tuanaiseo files PR #70 with a commit message spelling out the symlink attack in full. PR #71 filed minutes later with stronger parent-chain check. Both PRs sit open.

entries.label: STILL SHIPPING
entries.note: v1.4.0, v1.4.1, v1.5.0, v1.5.1 released while PRs #70 and #71 sit unaddressed. All four versions ship the vulnerable code.

entries.label: FIX SHIPS
entries.note: Maintainer batch-merges 10+ PRs including #70 and #71 within a 23-minute window. v1.6.0 tagged — 'Hardening release: hook crash fixes + symlink-safe flag writes.' Code fix is good. Batch-merge is bad.

entries.label: NO ADVISORY
entries.note: No GitHub Security Advisory created. No CVE. No SECURITY.md added. Users of v1.0.0-v1.5.1 receive no programmatic notification that they are running vulnerable code.

entries.label: SCAN
entries.note: This scan runs — 1 day after v1.6.0 release. Vulnerable versions (v1.0.0-v1.5.1) remain installable from the Releases page.

phase_4_structured_llm.timeline_intro: Eight events over 13 days. **The story is exploit-then-fix — but no disclosure.** Repo launched April 4 and went viral; a reporter found and described the symlink attack by April 10; the maintainer shipped 4 more vulnerable releases before merging the fix on April 15 in a 10-PR batch. The disclosure gap is the one still-open wound: no advisory, no SECURITY.md, no programmatic upgrade signal.

per_file.note: Fetches live main and writes to ~/.claude/hooks/
SessionStart hook — writes ~/.claude/.caveman-active. Symlink-safe in v1.6.0.
UserPromptSubmit hook — reads + writes ~/.claude/.caveman-active. Symlink-safe in v1.6.0.

executable_file_inventory.lead: ⚠ 3 Warning · ℹ 2 Info · ✓ 3 OK — 8 executable files cataloged. 3 need attention: install.sh (fetches live main) and two hook files (had pre-v1.6.0 symlink vuln, now fixed). 3 are OK. 2 are Info (CI-amplified rule source + README paste block).
executable_file_inventory.layer1_summary: 8 executable files: 2 hook JS files (symlink-safe in v1.6.0, previously vulnerable), 1 config JS module (security-critical), 1 install shell script (fetches live main), 1 uninstall shell script, 1 CI workflow (amplifies rule file to 4 agents), 1 agent rule source (17-line behavioral rule, CI-synced), and 1 README paste-block (7-line user-pasted rules).

layer2_entries.notes: Warning: live-main fetch, not pinned to any release tag. Writes to ~/.claude/hooks/*.
Runs every Claude Code session start. Symlink-safe in v1.6.0 via safeWriteFlag. Vulnerable in v1.0.0-v1.5.1.
Runs on every user prompt. Reads stdin (user prompt JSON). Both read and write paths symlink-safe in v1.6.0.
Security-critical module. Implements O_NOFOLLOW + temp+rename + symlink refusal. Watch in future diffs.
Uses env-var-passing pattern (not shell interpolation). Removes ~/.claude/hooks/caveman-* and rewrites settings.json.
Copies SKILL.md into 4 agent rule locations on push to main. contents:write scoped to repo. No pull_request_target. No SHA-pinned actions — uses actions/checkout@v4.
17 lines of behavioral rules. CI-synced to 4 always-on agent rule files. High leverage: 1 file → every user's always-on agent config on next CI run.
7-line block users paste into their agent's system prompt. Safe today; permanent consequence once pasted.

executable_file_inventory.closing_note: The two hook files (caveman-activate.js and caveman-mode-tracker.js) were the vulnerability sites pre-v1.6.0 — the symlink-unsafe fs.writeFileSync calls have been replaced with safeWriteFlag from caveman-config.js. The install.sh file remains a live-main fetch risk regardless of code version.

pr_sample_review.intro: **33 PRs reviewed — full coverage (under the 300-PR cap).** 6 PRs flagged as security-relevant, none had formal review. Two are the security fix itself (#70, #71). The other four are hook-touching PRs merged the same day in the batch without any approvals.

phase_4_structured_llm.coverage_intro: **33/33 PRs reviewed · 8/8 executables inspected · 0/6 distribution channels fully verified · 2 gaps noted.** All 33 merged PRs reviewed (full coverage under the 300-PR cap). Every executable file inspected. Distribution channels: 0 of 6 fully verified against source — method limitation, flagged under F1+F2.

6 of 19 — both Security-labeled PRs (#70, #71) plus 4 recent hook-touching PRs read in full
❌ Blocked — Dependabot explicitly disabled (HTTP 403), but runtime dep list is trivial
❌ Not indexed — API returned 404; repo too new for OSSF crawl

pr_target_usage.note: Zero pull_request_target usage in sync-skill.yml — rules out that class of attack

entries: Windows hook variants (install.ps1, uninstall.ps1, caveman-statusline.ps1) not inspected in equal depth to the Unix shell and Node.js files.
13 of 19 security-keyword PRs were classified from metadata only — their diffs were not read in full.

phase_4_structured_llm.evidence_intro: 10 command-backed claims. **Skip ahead to items marked ★ START HERE** — the vulnerability proof, the no-advisory confirmation, and the branch-protection check. Those three are the falsification criteria for the Critical verdict.

entries.claim: v1.6.0 fixes a real symlink vulnerability that existed in v1.0.0–v1.5.1
entries.result:
The SessionStart hook writes to `~/.claude/.caveman-active` using `fs.writeFileSync` on a predictable path. If that path is replaced with a symlink, Node will follow it and overwrite the symlink target. A local attacker (or another process running as the same user) could abuse this to modify unintended files writable by the user.

Affected files: caveman-activate.js, caveman-mode-tracker.js

Signed-off-by: tuanaiseo 221258316+tuanaiseo@users.noreply.github.com
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — vulnerability described in the reporter's own commit message; fix adds O_NOFOLLOW and symlink refusal; before/after diff confirms the unprotected write.

entries.claim: No GitHub Security Advisory was issued for the fix
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — empty array response means zero advisories published.

entries.claim: v1.6.0 was a 10+ PR batch merge committed at a single timestamp
entries.command:
gh pr list -R JuliusBrussee/caveman --state merged --limit 300 --json number,title,mergedAt \
  | jq '[.[] | select(.mergedAt == "2026-04-15T12:50:16Z")] | length'
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — 16 PRs share the same mergedAt timestamp, reflecting a synchronized batch merge.

entries.claim: 85% of merged PRs have no formal review
entries.command:
gh pr list -R JuliusBrussee/caveman --state merged --limit 300 --json reviewDecision \
  | jq '[.[] | select(.reviewDecision != null and .reviewDecision != "")] | length'
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — 5 of 33 merged PRs = 15% formal review rate.
Confirmed fact — 404 is authoritative here. The authenticated token has admin-read access and would return 403 if the issue were permissions; 404 means no rule exists.

entries.claim: Dependabot is disabled for this repository
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — the response distinguishes 'disabled' from a permissions error.
Confirmed fact — the hook package.json declares no dependencies; the only Python dep is the Anthropic SDK, only for the benchmark suite.

entries.claim: Maintainer is an established account, not a sockpuppet
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — account created 2022-04-21 (4 years old at time of scan), 42 public repos, 618 followers.

entries.claim: v1.6.0 hooks implement defense-in-depth against symlink attacks
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — the v1.6.0 code uses every defense expected for this class of vulnerability.
Confirmed fact — the only SECURITY.md is nested inside caveman-compress/ and covers a different concern (Snyk static-analysis rating), not a disclosure policy.

catalog_metadata.short_description: Claude Code plugin that compresses LLM output to caveman speech to cut ~65% of tokens. Ships as Claude Code plugin, Codex plugin, Gemini CLI extension, and agent rule files for Cursor/Windsurf/Cline/Copilot.
catalog_metadata.target_user: Claude Code users, AI agent developers
catalog_metadata.prior_scan_note: None — first scan; rename this file on re-run.

section_01.pill_row: ⚠ Action needed if installed < v1.6.0 · 3 steps
section_01.summary: **Most urgent:** if you installed caveman before 2026-04-15, upgrade now (Step 1). For new installers today (Step 2): code is fine but install method matters. Step 3 is what to ask the maintainer.

groups.headline: Historical vulnerability, disclosed through release notes only
groups.one_line_summary: Symlink bug shipped 11 days, fix landed in v1.6.0, no GitHub Security Advisory

items.claim: Symlink-based file-clobber vulnerability existed in all versions v1.0.0 through v1.5.1 (11 days, 2026-04-04 to 2026-04-15). Fixed in v1.6.0.
Unadvertised security fix — no advisory, no CVE, but release title reads 'Hardening release: hook crash fixes + symlink-safe flag writes.' Named in release notes, not in GitHub's advisory channel.
Security PR sat open for 5 days after the reporter explained the exact attack vector in the commit message.

groups.headline: Governance and distribution — no review gate, no pinning
groups.one_line_summary: 15% formal review rate, no branch protection, no CODEOWNERS, every install channel tracks live main

items.claim: 15% formal (5/33) and 36% any-review (12/33). 7-PR gap = informal review without formal decision. Security PRs #70 and #71 had zero review activity.
v1.6.0 'hardening release' is a 10+ PR batch merge committed at a single timestamp — security fix hidden among unrelated changes.
Every distribution channel fetches from live main or unpinned artifacts — no release assets, marketplace manifest says "source": "./", install scripts curl from main on both Linux and Windows.
No branch protection on main. No CODEOWNERS anywhere — no path-scoped review gate either. Dependabot disabled.

groups.headline: Positive signals — code quality, scope, maintainer legitimacy
groups.one_line_summary: v1.6.0 code is defensive, dep surface minimal, maintainer is an established 4-year account

items.claim: The v1.6.0 hardening code itself is thorough: O_NOFOLLOW, atomic temp-and-rename, whitelist-validated reads, size caps, symlink refusal. Windows PS1 parity confirmed.
Minimal runtime surface — 1 dependency (benchmarks only), zero network calls in hook files.
Established 4-year GitHub account with 42 public repos, 618 followers — not a sockpuppet.

editorial_caption.text: A twelve-day-old Claude Code plugin that shipped real local-privilege-escalation vulnerabilities before it got to a thousand stars — and fixed them without an advisory. This is the forensic record.

entries.what_we_observed: Classic branch protection on main returns 404. Rulesets: []. Rules on main: []. CODEOWNERS absent in all four standard locations. Owner is a User account (no org-level rulesets possible).
entries.what_this_means: When you install caveman, you are trusting JuliusBrussee's account security as tightly as you'd trust your own laptop's password. There is no automated gate that would catch a phished-credential push before it reaches your next install, and the hook files run on every Claude Code session.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: This does not mean the current v1.6.0 code is malicious. The finding is purely about the upstream process gap — any future compromise has no automated gate.

entries.what_we_observed: PR #70 commit message spells out the symlink attack in full. The hook wrote to a predictable path using fs.writeFileSync without any symlink checks. Affected versions: v1.0.0 through v1.5.1 (11 days live). Fix adds O_NOFOLLOW, atomic temp+rename, and symlink refusal.
entries.what_this_means: Any user who installed caveman between 2026-04-04 and 2026-04-14 ran vulnerable code. A local attacker or malicious process could have redirected the hook's flag file write to overwrite sensitive user files.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: This does not mean every user was exploited. Exploitation requires a local attacker or malicious co-process — it's not a remote code execution bug. v1.6.0 is clean.

entries.what_we_observed: Security-advisories API returns []. No SECURITY.md at repo root. Dependabot disabled (HTTP 403 with explicit message).
entries.what_this_means: Users of v1.0.0-v1.5.1 have no programmatic way to learn they should upgrade. There is no CVE, no GHSA, no advisory feed. Release notes are the only disclosure channel.
